notes:
này means “this” — something close to you (in space, time, or context).

It comes after the noun, not before.  

Often used with classifiers like cái, người, con, quyển depending on the noun.  
Opposite idea: đó / kia = “that” (farther away).

It can also be used alone to call someone’s attention (“hey”), and can sound a bit sharp depending on tone.

translation.en: I want to eat this dish.

sentenceBreakdowns: Em (I) + thích (like) + cái (thing) + này (this) + lắm (a lot)
notes_md:
**này** means **“this”** — something close to you (in space, time, or context).

It comes **after the noun**, not before.  

Often used with classifiers like **cái, người, con, quyển** depending on the noun.  
Opposite idea: **đó / kia** = “that” (farther away).

It can also be used alone to call someone’s attention (“hey”), and can sound a bit sharp depending on tone.

definitions.vi: Từ này được sử dụng để chỉ một cái gì đó gần với người nói.
definitions.es: Esta palabra se usa para referirse a algo que está cerca del hablante.
definitions.en: This word is used to refer to something that is close to the speaker.
